slides.title: The fruit that *eats like butter* but works like medicine.
slides.sub: Avocado is rich in monounsaturated fat, potassium and fiber. Three quiet allies for the aging heart.

slides.title: Fat that loves your heart
slides.body: Most of an avocado's fat is monounsaturated, the same kind found in olive oil. It *can support* healthier cholesterol.

slides.title: Swap, don't just add
slides.body: Trading saturated fat for this softer fat *is linked to* lower LDL, the cholesterol that hardens arteries over time.

slides.title: More than a banana
slides.body: One avocado carries more potassium than a banana. That mineral *can help* ease pressure on aging blood vessels.

slides.title: Steadier blood sugar
slides.body: Nearly ten grams of fiber slows digestion, so glucose rises gently. Steadier blood sugar *may help* protect your heart.

slides.title: Half a day is plenty
slides.body: Half an avocado on toast, salad or eggs is enough. Small, repeatable choices *compound* across decades.

slides.title: Eat for the *long game*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs the food, movement, steps and sleep behind a healthy heart, then turns them into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Avocado tastes like a treat but works like a tool for your heart. 🥑🌿

Monounsaturated fat, potassium and fiber can support healthier cholesterol and steadier blood sugar as you age. Save this for your next grocery run. 🔖

Vibe Health turns everyday food choices into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Avocado Is a Quiet Win for Your Heart
